commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Philo believes that the princess feigned him to be her own child; Moses denied that he was, and would not take advantage of this adoption. (Hebrews xi. 24.)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Not a word of Temple, not a word of Sacrifice, while all these Providences are taking place. And he was nourished in a barbarian house.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Not a word of Temple, not a word of Sacrifice, while all these Providences are taking place. And he was nourished in a barbarian house. And Moses was learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.
